articles.headline: India Unveils 'Amrit Kaal Green Hydrogen Export Hub' Initiative to Boost Green Economy
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the 'Amrit Kaal Green Hydrogen Export Hub' initiative, targeting significant production and export of green hydrogen by 2030.
• The initiative aims to leverage India's renewable energy potential to establish itself as a global leader in green hydrogen.
• Key components include developing dedicated infrastructure for production, storage, and export, alongside R&D in green hydrogen technologies.
• Financial incentives and policy support will be provided to domestic and international investors in the green hydrogen value chain.
• This move is expected to create numerous jobs, reduce carbon emissions, and enhance India's energy security.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy
Investment models
Environmental pollution and degradation
New and renewable energy

mainsPoints: Discuss India's strategic vision for green hydrogen in achieving energy independence and climate targets.
Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of becoming a green hydrogen export hub.
Examine the challenges in infrastructure development, technological adoption, and international market competition for green hydrogen.
Evaluate the role of government policies and private investment in scaling up green hydrogen production.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with solar panels and wind turbines in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, making up roughly 75% of all baryonic mass.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Core Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Suggests Review on Data Localization
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered its much-anticipated verdict on petitions challenging various provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The Court largely upheld the constitutional validity of the Act's core framework for data privacy, affirming the right to privacy as a fundamental right.
• However, it directed the government to review certain aspects related to mandatory data localization for 'significant data fiduciaries' and the breadth of exemptions granted to government agencies.
• The judgment emphasized the need for a balanced approach between national security, economic interests, and individual privacy rights.
• This ruling is expected to have significant implications for tech companies, data fiduciaries, and individual users in India.

Government policies and interventions
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Role of civil services in a democracy
prelimsPoints: DPDP Act, 2023 aims to protect digital personal data.
Supreme Court upheld core provisions, emphasizing right to privacy.
Court suggested review on data localization for significant data fiduciaries and government exemptions.
The judgment balances national security, economic interests, and individual privacy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the DPDP Act for individual privacy, businesses, and government surveillance.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities presented by data localization policies in the context of digital economy and national sovereignty.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the DPDP Act in safeguarding fundamental rights while promoting digital innovation.
Examine the role of the judiciary in interpreting and shaping data protection laws in India.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and law books placed on a wooden desk, symbolizing judicial proceedings.
articles.trivia: The 'right to be forgotten' is a key provision under the DPDP Act, allowing individuals to request the deletion of their personal data under certain conditions.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Evolving Inflation Dynamics and Global Slowdown Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision was influenced by evolving inflation dynamics, particularly food inflation, and global economic uncertainties including geopolitical tensions and slower growth projections.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the central bank's commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• The MPC also maintained the standing deposit facility (SDF) rate at 6.25% and the marginal standing facility (MSF) rate and bank rate at 6.75%.
• Analysts suggest this pause indicates a watchful approach, waiting for more definitive signs of sustained inflation moderation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI's MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Decision driven by inflation dynamics and global economic uncertainties.
SDF rate remains at 6.25%, MSF rate and bank rate at 6.75%.
RBI aims to align inflation with target while supporting growth.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the repo rate and its potential impact on India's economic growth and inflation trajectory.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the need to support economic recovery amidst global headwinds.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the current monetary policy stance in achieving price stability and financial stability.
Examine the role of food inflation and global supply chain disruptions in shaping India's monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo on a building facade.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Maritime Security, Digital Cooperation, and Enhanced Trade
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded in Jakarta, with leaders reaffirming their commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership.
• Key discussions centered on strengthening cooperation in maritime security, counter-terrorism, and regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
• A new 'Framework for Digital Cooperation' was adopted, aiming to foster collaboration in emerging technologies, cybersecurity, and digital infrastructure.
• Both sides emphasized boosting trade and investment, with a review of the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) agreement expected to be fast-tracked.
• The summit underscored India's 'Act East' policy and its growing strategic alignment with the ASEAN bloc amidst evolving geopolitical landscapes.

Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.

mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-ASEAN strategic partnership for regional stability and India's economic integration with Southeast Asia.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in enhancing maritime security and counter-terrorism cooperation between India and ASEAN nations.
Evaluate the potential impact of the 'Framework for Digital Cooperation' and AIFTA review on India's digital economy and trade relations.
Examine how India's 'Act East' policy aligns with its broader geopolitical objectives in the Indo-Pacific region.

articles.trivia: ASEAN (Association of Southeast Asian Nations) was established on 8 August 1967 in Bangkok, Thailand, with the signing of the ASEAN Declaration (Bangkok Declaration).

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype for Future Space Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful maiden test flight of its advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• The autonomous landing experiment demonstrated critical technologies for a fully reusable orbital vehicle, a key step towards reducing space launch costs significantly.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system capable of carrying heavier payloads.
• This achievement places India among a select few nations developing such sophisticated space technology, promising cost-effective access to space.
• Future phases will focus on orbital re-entry, hypersonic flight, and advanced propulsion systems for full operational capability.

Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested an advanced RLV prototype.

mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its future aspirations in space exploration.
Analyze the economic benefits and technological challenges associated with developing and deploying reusable launch systems.
Compare India's RLV program with similar initiatives by other major spacefaring nations.
Examine how indigenous development of advanced space technologies like RLV contributes to national self-reliance and global competitiveness.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype during atmospheric re-entry.
articles.trivia: The first RLV-TD, named 'Pushpak', was successfully tested by ISRO in 2016, performing an atmospheric re-entry experiment.

articles.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches 'Coastal Ecosystem Restoration Project' for Climate Resilience
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) launched a new 'Coastal Ecosystem Restoration Project' (CERP) across nine coastal states and four Union Territories.
• The multi-year project aims to restore degraded coastal ecosystems, including mangroves, coral reefs, and salt marshes, vital for climate change adaptation and biodiversity conservation.
• CERP will involve community participation, sustainable livelihood generation for coastal populations, and integration of traditional knowledge in conservation efforts.
• Funding for the project will come from both central government allocations and international climate finance mechanisms.
• This initiative is critical for protecting vulnerable coastal communities from rising sea levels, extreme weather events, and coastal erosion.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Climate change.

Analyze the multi-faceted approach of CERP, including ecological restoration, community involvement, and livelihood generation.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale coastal restoration projects, such as funding, land acquisition, and scientific expertise.
Evaluate the role of international cooperation and traditional knowledge in successful coastal conservation efforts.
articles.imageDescription: A lush mangrove forest along a coastline, with calm waters reflecting the greenery.
articles.trivia: Mangrove forests are highly effective 'blue carbon' ecosystems, capable of sequestering carbon at a rate several times higher than terrestrial forests.

articles.headline: Government Expands 'Skill India Plus' Programme to Empower Over 10 Million Rural Youth
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship announced a significant expansion of its flagship 'Skill India Plus' programme.
• The enhanced scheme targets to upskill and reskill over 10 million rural youth in the next two years, focusing on emerging sectors like AI, data analytics, green jobs, and digital marketing.
• The program integrates vocational training with entrepreneurial development, providing mentorship and access to micro-finance for start-ups.
• Emphasis is placed on industry-led curriculum development and partnerships with private sector companies for guaranteed placements.
• This initiative aims to bridge the skill gap in rural areas, reduce urban migration, and foster self-employment among the youth.

Examine how public-private partnerships can enhance the effectiveness and reach of skill development programs.
articles.imageDescription: A group of young men and women in a training center learning computer skills.
articles.trivia: The Skill India Mission was launched in 2015 to train over 40 crore (400 million) people in India in different skills by 2022.

articles.headline: BRO Completes Strategic Sela Tunnel Bypass in Arunachal Pradesh, Enhancing Border Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the completion of a crucial bypass road connected to the strategically vital Sela Tunnel in Arunachal Pradesh.
• This bypass significantly reduces travel time and provides all-weather connectivity to Tawang, a critical area near the India-China border.
• The Sela Tunnel project, including its bypass, is part of India's broader strategy to enhance infrastructure development along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• Improved connectivity will facilitate faster movement of troops and supplies, bolstering national security and regional development.
• The engineering marvel involved overcoming challenging high-altitude terrain and adverse weather conditions.

Infrastructure: Roads
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.

mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of infrastructure development, such as the Sela Tunnel and its bypass, for India's national security along its northern borders.

Examine how enhanced border infrastructure contributes to India's defense preparedness and geopolitical standing.
articles.imageDescription: View of the Sela Tunnel entrance amidst snow-capped mountains in Arunachal Pradesh.
articles.trivia: The Sela Tunnel, at an altitude of 13,000 feet, is the world's longest bi-lane tunnel above 13,000 feet.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms for Prison Modernization and Inmate Rehabilitation
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Home Affairs submitted its report on 'Prison Reforms and Modernization' to the Lok Sabha.
• The report highlighted issues such as overcrowding, understaffing, lack of proper healthcare, and inadequate rehabilitation programs in Indian prisons.
• Key recommendations include increasing the budget for prison infrastructure, adopting advanced surveillance technologies, and enhancing training for prison staff.
• The panel also advocated for greater use of alternative dispute resolution mechanisms and community service to reduce the burden on correctional facilities.
• Emphasis was placed on ensuring human rights of prisoners and integrating them back into society through vocational training and psychological support.

articles.trivia: Prisons are a 'State Subject' under Entry 4 of List II of the Seventh Schedule to the Constitution of India.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for UN Peacekeeping Operations, Focus on Women Peacekeepers
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its strong commitment to United Nations Peacekeeping Operations (UNPKOs) at a high-level ministerial meeting in New York.
• The External Affairs Minister announced an increased financial contribution and pledged to deploy more women peacekeepers in various UN missions.
• India emphasized the need for greater safety and security for peacekeepers, robust mandates, and political solutions to conflicts.
• As one of the largest troop-contributing countries, India highlighted its historical role and expertise in diverse peacekeeping environments.
• The move underscores India's dedication to multilateralism and its growing influence in global security affairs.

articles.imageDescription: Indian UN peacekeepers on patrol in a conflict zone.
articles.trivia: India has contributed over 275,000 troops to UN peacekeeping missions since the 1950s, with over 170 Indian peacekeepers having made the supreme sacrifice.

articles.headline: Government Boosts Incentives for Agricultural Processed Food Exports to Enhance Farmer Income
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry announced a revised policy framework to significantly boost the export of agricultural processed food products.
• The new policy includes enhanced financial incentives, streamlined export procedures, and support for developing specialized infrastructure like cold chains and food testing labs.
• A key objective is to increase farmer income by connecting them directly to global value chains and encouraging value addition to agricultural produce.
• Focus sectors include organic produce, ready-to-eat meals, dairy products, and specialty crops, with targeted market promotion campaigns.
• This initiative aligns with the government's goal of doubling farmers' income and making India a global food processing hub.

articles.trivia: India is one of the world's largest producers of several agricultural commodities, yet its share in global processed food exports remains relatively low.

articles.headline: New Study Reveals Alarming Acceleration in Himalayan Glacier Melt Rates, Posing Water Security Threat
articles.explainer:
• A collaborative study by Indian and international researchers published in 'Nature Geoscience' indicates an alarming acceleration in the melting rate of Himalayan glaciers.
• The study, based on satellite imagery and ground observations over the past two decades, shows glaciers are losing mass at a rate significantly higher than previously estimated.
• This rapid retreat poses a severe long-term threat to water security for millions of people dependent on Himalayan rivers for drinking water, irrigation, and hydropower.
• The researchers attributed the accelerated melt primarily to rising global temperatures and increased black carbon deposition.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for enhanced climate action and regional cooperation on water resource management.
gsSubTopics: Important Geophysical phenomena such as earthquakes, Tsunami, Volcanic activity, cyclone etc., geographical features and their location-changes in critical geographical features (including waterbodies and ice-caps) and in flora and fauna and the effects of such changes.
Climate change.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Study in 'Nature Geoscience' reports accelerated Himalayan glacier melt.

mainsPoints: Analyze the multi-dimensional impact of accelerated Himalayan glacier melt on India's water security, agriculture, and hydropower generation.
Discuss the scientific evidence and factors contributing to the rapid glacial retreat in the Himalayas, including anthropogenic influences.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current national and international strategies to mitigate climate change and adapt to its consequences in the Himalayan region.
Examine the geopolitical implications of water scarcity in the Himalayan river basins and the need for regional cooperation.
articles.imageDescription: A panoramic view of a rapidly melting Himalayan glacier with visible crevasses and meltwater streams.
articles.trivia: The Himalayas are often referred to as the 'Third Pole' because they contain the largest ice mass outside the polar regions.

articles.headline: Indigenous Dengue Vaccine Shows Promising Results in Phase 3 Clinical Trials, Nearing Approval
articles.explainer:
• An indigenously developed dengue vaccine candidate, developed by the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) in collaboration with a private pharmaceutical firm, announced successful Phase 3 clinical trial results.
• The vaccine demonstrated high efficacy against all four serotypes of the dengue virus, with a strong safety profile across diverse age groups.
• This breakthrough is critical for India, which bears a significant burden of dengue cases annually, especially during monsoon seasons.
• Regulatory approval is anticipated within the next year, paving the way for mass production and nationwide immunization campaigns.
• The development highlights India's growing capabilities in vaccine research and development, contributing to global health security.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Evaluate the potential impact of widespread dengue vaccination on disease burden, healthcare costs, and economic productivity.
Examine the role of public-private partnerships in accelerating vaccine development and ensuring equitable access.
articles.imageDescription: A scientist in a lab coat holding a vial with a vaccine, with a microscope in the background.
articles.trivia: Dengue is a mosquito-borne tropical disease caused by the dengue virus, with symptoms typically including fever, headache, vomiting, muscle and joint pains, and a characteristic skin rash.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020 Review Highlights Progress in Digital Infrastructure and Teacher Training
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released its annual review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The report highlighted significant progress in establishing digital education infrastructure, including the expansion of online learning platforms and e-content development.
• Substantial strides have also been made in teacher training and capacity building programs, focusing on pedagogical innovations and technology integration.
• Challenges remain in ensuring equitable access to digital resources in remote areas and addressing teacher vacancies in certain subjects.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century, promoting holistic and multidisciplinary learning.

articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern classroom interacting with digital learning tools, with a teacher assisting.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 34-year-old National Policy on Education (NPE) of 1986.

articles.headline: India to Host International Conference on Cyber Warfare Ethics and Responsible AI Development
articles.explainer:
• New Delhi is set to host a major international conference on 'Cyber Warfare Ethics and Responsible Artificial Intelligence Development' next month.
• The conference will bring together global experts, policymakers, and military strategists to discuss the ethical dilemmas and regulatory frameworks surrounding cyber warfare and autonomous weapons systems.
• Key themes include the application of international humanitarian law in cyberspace, accountability for cyber attacks, and the responsible development and deployment of AI in defense.
• India aims to play a leading role in shaping global norms and standards for cyber security and AI governance, advocating for a human-centric approach.
• This initiative highlights India's growing stature in critical and emerging technologies and its commitment to responsible global digital governance.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life

articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of interconnected digital circuits overlaid with a globe, symbolizing global cyber security.
articles.trivia: The 'Tallinn Manual' is a widely recognized academic study on how existing international law applies to cyber warfare.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Report Advocates for Enhanced Financial Autonomy for Urban Local Bodies
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released a comprehensive report titled 'Reimagining Urban Governance: Pathways to Empowering Local Bodies' advocating for greater financial autonomy for Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
• The report highlights the critical role of ULBs in urban development and service delivery but points out their over-reliance on state and central grants.
• Recommendations include increasing property tax collection efficiency, enabling ULBs to issue municipal bonds, and providing access to innovative financing mechanisms.
• It also suggested capacity building for ULB officials in financial management and urban planning.
• Empowering ULBs financially is deemed essential for addressing rapid urbanization challenges and achieving sustainable urban development goals.

articles.trivia: The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, gave constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies in India.

articles.headline: India Calls for Review of Indus Waters Treaty Provisions Amidst Climate Change Concerns
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its call for a review of certain provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing the impact of climate change on water flows and infrastructure development needs.
• During bilateral talks, Indian officials emphasized the need for a more equitable and dynamic framework to address contemporary water management challenges.
• Pakistan, while acknowledging climate impacts, maintained its stance on strict adherence to the existing treaty provisions.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of the Indus river system between the two countries.
• India's push for a review stems from its increasing water demands and the necessity to build more storage and hydropower projects on the western rivers.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.

mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty and its role in India-Pakistan relations.
Discuss the reasons behind India's call for a review of the IWT, particularly in light of climate change and increasing water demands.
Examine the potential implications of a treaty review for bilateral relations and regional water security.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities for cooperative water management between India and Pakistan in the Indus basin.
articles.imageDescription: A satellite map showing the Indus River system flowing through India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is often cited as one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Researchers Discover Three New Species of Frogs in the Western Ghats Biodiversity Hotspot
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists announced the discovery of three new species of frogs in the highly biodiverse Western Ghats region of India.
• The new species, belonging to the genus 'Nyctibatrachus' (commonly known as night frogs), were identified through extensive fieldwork and genetic analysis.
• This discovery further underscores the ecological richness and unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Researchers emphasized the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts in the region, as these habitats are increasingly threatened by deforestation, climate change, and human encroachment.
• The findings highlight the importance of continued taxonomic research in understanding and protecting India's natural heritage.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.

mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the implications of discovering new species in the region.
Analyze the major threats to the biodiversity of the Western Ghats and the measures required for their effective conservation.
Evaluate the role of taxonomic research in understanding and protecting India's natural heritage.
Examine the balance between developmental activities and environmental conservation in ecologically sensitive regions like the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up photograph of a small, distinctively colored frog found in a forest habitat.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains and are recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

articles.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records Robust Growth, Contributes Significantly to GDP and Employment
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism released its annual report, indicating robust growth in India's tourism sector, surpassing pre-pandemic levels.
• The sector recorded a significant increase in both domestic and international tourist arrivals, leading to a substantial contribution to the national GDP and employment generation.
• Government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and 'Swadesh Darshan' schemes, along with improved connectivity and infrastructure, were credited for the surge.
• Focus areas for future growth include sustainable tourism, eco-tourism, medical tourism, and promoting lesser-known destinations.
• The strong performance underscores the tourism sector's potential as a key driver for economic recovery and cultural exchange.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.

articles.headline: POSHAN Abhiyaan Review Shows Progress in Reducing Malnutrition, Focus on Maternal and Child Health
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development released a progress report on the National Nutrition Mission (POSHAN Abhiyaan), indicating positive trends in reducing malnutrition indicators.
• The report highlighted improvements in stunting, wasting, and anemia among children and women, attributing success to integrated health and nutrition services.
• Key interventions included enhanced supplementary nutrition, early childhood care, and health education through Anganwadi centers.
• Challenges persist in reaching remote populations and ensuring sustained behavioral change regarding dietary practices.
• The mission continues to prioritize maternal and child health, emphasizing the first 1000 days of a child's life for optimal development.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Amrit Kaal Green Hydrogen Export Hub' initiative, consider the following statements:
1. Its primary objective is to establish India as a global leader in the production and export of green hydrogen by 2030.
2. The initiative focuses solely on developing infrastructure for hydrogen production, excluding storage and export facilities.
3. Green hydrogen is produced by the electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The initiative aims to make India a global leader in green hydrogen production and export by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect: The initiative includes developing dedicated infrastructure for production, storage, and export. Statement 3 is correct: Green hydrogen is indeed produced by the electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources, differentiating it from grey or blue hydrogen.

prelimsMocks.question:
The recent Supreme Court verdict on the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023, while largely upholding its validity, suggested a review on which of the following aspects?
1. The definition of 'personal data'.
2. Mandatory data localization for 'significant data fiduciaries'.
3. The breadth of exemptions granted to government agencies.
4. The composition of the Data Protection Board.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court directed the government to review certain aspects related to mandatory data localization for 'significant data fiduciaries' and the breadth of exemptions granted to government agencies. It largely upheld the core framework, implying that the definition of personal data and the composition of the Data Protection Board were not the primary points of suggested review in this specific verdict.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's contribution to UN Peacekeeping Operations:
1. India is one of the largest troop-contributing countries to UNPKOs.
2. India has pledged to deploy more women peacekeepers in various UN missions.
3. India's commitment to UNPKOs began only after the end of the Cold War.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: India is indeed one of the largest troop-contributing countries. Statement 2 is correct: India has pledged to deploy more women peacekeepers. Statement 3 is incorrect: India has been contributing to UN peacekeeping missions since the 1950s, long before the end of the Cold War.

prelimsMocks.question: The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) primarily governs the sharing of the Indus river system between India and Pakistan. Which of the following rivers are designated as 'Western Rivers' under the treaty, whose waters are largely allocated to Pakistan?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Under the Indus Waters Treaty, the 'Western Rivers' are Indus, Jhelum, and Chenab, whose waters are largely for Pakistan's unrestricted use. The 'Eastern Rivers' are Ravi, Beas, and Sutlej, whose waters are allocated to India.

GS1.headline: New Study Reveals Alarming Acceleration in Himalayan Glacier Melt Rates, Posing Water Security Threat
GS1.explainer:
• A collaborative study by Indian and international researchers published in 'Nature Geoscience' indicates an alarming acceleration in the melting rate of Himalayan glaciers.
• The study, based on satellite imagery and ground observations over the past two decades, shows glaciers are losing mass at a rate significantly higher than previously estimated.
• This rapid retreat poses a severe long-term threat to water security for millions of people dependent on Himalayan rivers for drinking water, irrigation, and hydropower.
• The researchers attributed the accelerated melt primarily to rising global temperatures and increased black carbon deposition.
• The findings underscore the urgent need for enhanced climate action and regional cooperation on water resource management.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Core Provisions of Digital Personal Data Protection Act, Suggests Review on Data Localization
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered its much-anticipated verdict on petitions challenging various provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023.
• The Court largely upheld the constitutional validity of the Act's core framework for data privacy, affirming the right to privacy as a fundamental right.
• However, it directed the government to review certain aspects related to mandatory data localization for 'significant data fiduciaries' and the breadth of exemptions granted to government agencies.
• The judgment emphasized the need for a balanced approach between national security, economic interests, and individual privacy rights.
• This ruling is expected to have significant implications for tech companies, data fiduciaries, and individual users in India.

mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the DPDP Act for individual privacy, businesses, and government surveillance.

GS2.trivia: The 'right to be forgotten' is a key provision under the DPDP Act, allowing individuals to request the deletion of their personal data under certain conditions.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Summit Concludes with Focus on Maritime Security, Digital Cooperation, and Enhanced Trade
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Summit concluded in Jakarta, with leaders reaffirming their commitment to a comprehensive strategic partnership.
• Key discussions centered on strengthening cooperation in maritime security, counter-terrorism, and regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
• A new 'Framework for Digital Cooperation' was adopted, aiming to foster collaboration in emerging technologies, cybersecurity, and digital infrastructure.
• Both sides emphasized boosting trade and investment, with a review of the ASEAN-India Free Trade Area (AIFTA) agreement expected to be fast-tracked.
• The summit underscored India's 'Act East' policy and its growing strategic alignment with the ASEAN bloc amidst evolving geopolitical landscapes.

GS2.headline: Government Expands 'Skill India Plus' Programme to Empower Over 10 Million Rural Youth
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship announced a significant expansion of its flagship 'Skill India Plus' programme.
• The enhanced scheme targets to upskill and reskill over 10 million rural youth in the next two years, focusing on emerging sectors like AI, data analytics, green jobs, and digital marketing.
• The program integrates vocational training with entrepreneurial development, providing mentorship and access to micro-finance for start-ups.
• Emphasis is placed on industry-led curriculum development and partnerships with private sector companies for guaranteed placements.
• This initiative aims to bridge the skill gap in rural areas, reduce urban migration, and foster self-employment among the youth.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Panel Recommends Comprehensive Reforms for Prison Modernization and Inmate Rehabilitation
GS2.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Home Affairs submitted its report on 'Prison Reforms and Modernization' to the Lok Sabha.
• The report highlighted issues such as overcrowding, understaffing, lack of proper healthcare, and inadequate rehabilitation programs in Indian prisons.
• Key recommendations include increasing the budget for prison infrastructure, adopting advanced surveillance technologies, and enhancing training for prison staff.
• The panel also advocated for greater use of alternative dispute resolution mechanisms and community service to reduce the burden on correctional facilities.
• Emphasis was placed on ensuring human rights of prisoners and integrating them back into society through vocational training and psychological support.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for UN Peacekeeping Operations, Focus on Women Peacekeepers
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its strong commitment to United Nations Peacekeeping Operations (UNPKOs) at a high-level ministerial meeting in New York.
• The External Affairs Minister announced an increased financial contribution and pledged to deploy more women peacekeepers in various UN missions.
• India emphasized the need for greater safety and security for peacekeepers, robust mandates, and political solutions to conflicts.
• As one of the largest troop-contributing countries, India highlighted its historical role and expertise in diverse peacekeeping environments.
• The move underscores India's dedication to multilateralism and its growing influence in global security affairs.

GS2.headline: NITI Aayog Report Advocates for Enhanced Financial Autonomy for Urban Local Bodies
GS2.explainer:
• NITI Aayog released a comprehensive report titled 'Reimagining Urban Governance: Pathways to Empowering Local Bodies' advocating for greater financial autonomy for Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
• The report highlights the critical role of ULBs in urban development and service delivery but points out their over-reliance on state and central grants.
• Recommendations include increasing property tax collection efficiency, enabling ULBs to issue municipal bonds, and providing access to innovative financing mechanisms.
• It also suggested capacity building for ULB officials in financial management and urban planning.
• Empowering ULBs financially is deemed essential for addressing rapid urbanization challenges and achieving sustainable urban development goals.

GS2.headline: India Calls for Review of Indus Waters Treaty Provisions Amidst Climate Change Concerns
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its call for a review of certain provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing the impact of climate change on water flows and infrastructure development needs.
• During bilateral talks, Indian officials emphasized the need for a more equitable and dynamic framework to address contemporary water management challenges.
• Pakistan, while acknowledging climate impacts, maintained its stance on strict adherence to the existing treaty provisions.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of the Indus river system between the two countries.
• India's push for a review stems from its increasing water demands and the necessity to build more storage and hydropower projects on the western rivers.

GS3.headline: India Unveils 'Amrit Kaal Green Hydrogen Export Hub' Initiative to Boost Green Economy
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the 'Amrit Kaal Green Hydrogen Export Hub' initiative, targeting significant production and export of green hydrogen by 2030.
• The initiative aims to leverage India's renewable energy potential to establish itself as a global leader in green hydrogen.
• Key components include developing dedicated infrastructure for production, storage, and export, alongside R&D in green hydrogen technologies.
• Financial incentives and policy support will be provided to domestic and international investors in the green hydrogen value chain.
• This move is expected to create numerous jobs, reduce carbon emissions, and enhance India's energy security.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% Amidst Evolving Inflation Dynamics and Global Slowdown Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive review.
• The decision was influenced by evolving inflation dynamics, particularly food inflation, and global economic uncertainties including geopolitical tensions and slower growth projections.
• RBI Governor Shaktikanta Das reiterated the central bank's commitment to withdrawing accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• The MPC also maintained the standing deposit facility (SDF) rate at 6.25% and the marginal standing facility (MSF) rate and bank rate at 6.75%.
• Analysts suggest this pause indicates a watchful approach, waiting for more definitive signs of sustained inflation moderation.

Examine the role of food inflation and global supply chain disruptions in shaping India's monetary policy decisions.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India logo on a building facade.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype for Future Space Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful maiden test flight of its advanced Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) prototype from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• The autonomous landing experiment demonstrated critical technologies for a fully reusable orbital vehicle, a key step towards reducing space launch costs significantly.
• The RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) project aims to develop a two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) reusable launch system capable of carrying heavier payloads.
• This achievement places India among a select few nations developing such sophisticated space technology, promising cost-effective access to space.
• Future phases will focus on orbital re-entry, hypersonic flight, and advanced propulsion systems for full operational capability.

GS3.trivia: The first RLV-TD, named 'Pushpak', was successfully tested by ISRO in 2016, performing an atmospheric re-entry experiment.

GS3.headline: Ministry of Environment Launches 'Coastal Ecosystem Restoration Project' for Climate Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) launched a new 'Coastal Ecosystem Restoration Project' (CERP) across nine coastal states and four Union Territories.
• The multi-year project aims to restore degraded coastal ecosystems, including mangroves, coral reefs, and salt marshes, vital for climate change adaptation and biodiversity conservation.
• CERP will involve community participation, sustainable livelihood generation for coastal populations, and integration of traditional knowledge in conservation efforts.
• Funding for the project will come from both central government allocations and international climate finance mechanisms.
• This initiative is critical for protecting vulnerable coastal communities from rising sea levels, extreme weather events, and coastal erosion.

GS3.trivia: Mangrove forests are highly effective 'blue carbon' ecosystems, capable of sequestering carbon at a rate several times higher than terrestrial forests.

GS3.headline: BRO Completes Strategic Sela Tunnel Bypass in Arunachal Pradesh, Enhancing Border Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the completion of a crucial bypass road connected to the strategically vital Sela Tunnel in Arunachal Pradesh.
• This bypass significantly reduces travel time and provides all-weather connectivity to Tawang, a critical area near the India-China border.
• The Sela Tunnel project, including its bypass, is part of India's broader strategy to enhance infrastructure development along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
• Improved connectivity will facilitate faster movement of troops and supplies, bolstering national security and regional development.
• The engineering marvel involved overcoming challenging high-altitude terrain and adverse weather conditions.

GS3.trivia: The Sela Tunnel, at an altitude of 13,000 feet, is the world's longest bi-lane tunnel above 13,000 feet.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts Incentives for Agricultural Processed Food Exports to Enhance Farmer Income
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry announced a revised policy framework to significantly boost the export of agricultural processed food products.
• The new policy includes enhanced financial incentives, streamlined export procedures, and support for developing specialized infrastructure like cold chains and food testing labs.
• A key objective is to increase farmer income by connecting them directly to global value chains and encouraging value addition to agricultural produce.
• Focus sectors include organic produce, ready-to-eat meals, dairy products, and specialty crops, with targeted market promotion campaigns.
• This initiative aligns with the government's goal of doubling farmers' income and making India a global food processing hub.

GS3.headline: Indigenous Dengue Vaccine Shows Promising Results in Phase 3 Clinical Trials, Nearing Approval
GS3.explainer:
• An indigenously developed dengue vaccine candidate, developed by the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) in collaboration with a private pharmaceutical firm, announced successful Phase 3 clinical trial results.
• The vaccine demonstrated high efficacy against all four serotypes of the dengue virus, with a strong safety profile across diverse age groups.
• This breakthrough is critical for India, which bears a significant burden of dengue cases annually, especially during monsoon seasons.
• Regulatory approval is anticipated within the next year, paving the way for mass production and nationwide immunization campaigns.
• The development highlights India's growing capabilities in vaccine research and development, contributing to global health security.

GS3.trivia: Dengue is a mosquito-borne tropical disease caused by the dengue virus, with symptoms typically including fever, headache, vomiting, muscle and joint pains, and a characteristic skin rash.

GS3.headline: India to Host International Conference on Cyber Warfare Ethics and Responsible AI Development
GS3.explainer:
• New Delhi is set to host a major international conference on 'Cyber Warfare Ethics and Responsible Artificial Intelligence Development' next month.
• The conference will bring together global experts, policymakers, and military strategists to discuss the ethical dilemmas and regulatory frameworks surrounding cyber warfare and autonomous weapons systems.
• Key themes include the application of international humanitarian law in cyberspace, accountability for cyber attacks, and the responsible development and deployment of AI in defense.
• India aims to play a leading role in shaping global norms and standards for cyber security and AI governance, advocating for a human-centric approach.
• This initiative highlights India's growing stature in critical and emerging technologies and its commitment to responsible global digital governance.

GS3.headline: Researchers Discover Three New Species of Frogs in the Western Ghats Biodiversity Hotspot
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists announced the discovery of three new species of frogs in the highly biodiverse Western Ghats region of India.
• The new species, belonging to the genus 'Nyctibatrachus' (commonly known as night frogs), were identified through extensive fieldwork and genetic analysis.
• This discovery further underscores the ecological richness and unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Researchers emphasized the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts in the region, as these habitats are increasingly threatened by deforestation, climate change, and human encroachment.
• The findings highlight the importance of continued taxonomic research in understanding and protecting India's natural heritage.

GS3.headline: India's Tourism Sector Records Robust Growth, Contributes Significantly to GDP and Employment
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Tourism released its annual report, indicating robust growth in India's tourism sector, surpassing pre-pandemic levels.
• The sector recorded a significant increase in both domestic and international tourist arrivals, leading to a substantial contribution to the national GDP and employment generation.
• Government initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and 'Swadesh Darshan' schemes, along with improved connectivity and infrastructure, were credited for the surge.
• Focus areas for future growth include sustainable tourism, eco-tourism, medical tourism, and promoting lesser-known destinations.
• The strong performance underscores the tourism sector's potential as a key driver for economic recovery and cultural exchange.
